title: cymbidium aloifolium var. pubescens
intro: Cymbidium aloifolium var. pubescens, also known as the hairy cymbidium, is a type of orchid plant characterized by its hairy leaves and stems. The hairy cymbidium has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including antibacterial and anti-inflammatory effects. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and soothing skin irritations. Herbal preparations of the hairy cymbidium, such as infusions and extracts, can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
